label: Platform
description: The whole platform — all services visible as actors. Describes cross-service topology, domain ownership, and shared infrastructure. Primary audience: architects, tech leads, engineers onboarding to a new system.

label: Service Interaction
description: How services talk to each other — cross-service business flows and choreography. Captures business use cases that cross service boundaries, the actors involved, and the event/API sequences between them. Primary audience: engineers designing cross-service features, architects auditing coupling.

label: Service Logic
description: Internal business logic of a single service. Describes what the service does in business terms, and optionally how the code routes internally. Below this layer is code — AI territory that engineers do not need to audit. Primary audience: engineers auditing AI-generated logic, AI implementing code from the business description.
